title: Light Slovakian Cabbage Flatbread
description: A savory low calorie flatbread with tender sauerkraut, crispy puffed layers, and a hint of spicy cajun seasoning that echoes nostalgic Slovak flavors.
instructions: Preheat oven to 220°C and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a bowl mix flour, salt, baking powder and water until a sticky dough forms.
Knead dough on a lightly floured surface for 2 minutes, then roll into a 30cm square.
Brush one side of the dough with olive oil and sprinkle evenly with paprika and cayenne.
Spread shredded cabbage and sliced onion over the seasoned side, then drizzle lemon juice and sprinkle dill.
Fold the dough over the filling, pinch edges to seal, and cut into six equal wedges.
Place wedges cut-side down on the prepared sheet and bake for 15 minutes until golden and crispy.
Flip each wedge, bake another 10 minutes until fully puffed and puffed edges.
Remove from oven, let cool on a rack for 5 minutes before serving.
Garnish with extra dill if desired and serve warm.
